India isn’t just using AI anymore — it’s building it. 🇮🇳
From career intelligence and multilingual language models to healthcare, marketing, and enterprise automation, Indian entrepreneurs are creating AI products that solve real-world problems and make a global impact.
We often talk about ChatGPT, Midjourney, and the latest AI breakthroughs but how often do we stop to look at what’s being built right here in India?
The answer is: not often enough.
Indian founders are no longer just adopting artificial intelligence — they’re shaping its future. Across industries, homegrown AI startups are helping businesses hire smarter, communicate in multiple languages, automate complex workflows, improve customer experiences, and unlock new opportunities at scale. They may not always dominate global headlines, but they’re delivering meaningful innovation where it matters most.
In this article, we’ve curated 10 of the most impactful AI tools founded by Indian entrepreneurs in 2026 exploring what they do, why they matter, and the real-world problems they’re solving
Press enter or click to view image in full size

1. Redrob AI — India’s AI-Powered Career & Talent Intelligence Platform
Founded by: Felix Kim
Category: Career Intelligence | Talent Acquisition | B2B Sales Intelligence
Redrob AI is India’s professional AI platform built to help businesses and professionals execute work faster from a single AI-powered workspace. It brings together hiring, career growth, prospecting, research, and productivity, eliminating the need to switch between multiple tools.
Whether you’re discovering your next opportunity, hiring top talent, identifying decision-makers, or streamlining everyday workflows, Redrob AI combines AI-powered search, automation, and business intelligence into one unified system.
What Redrob AI does:
For professionals, Redrob AI simplifies job discovery, resume optimization, interview preparation, and career growth with AI-powered assistance. For businesses, it provides access to 700M+ professional profiles, company intelligence, verified decision-makers, and AI-powered search to accelerate hiring, sales, and go-to-market execution from one platform.
Use Cases:

• For Students & Job Seekers: Discover relevant jobs, optimize resumes, and prepare for interviews with AI-powered career assistance.

• For Recruiters & HR Teams: Source, assess, and rank qualified candidates using AI-powered talent intelligence.

• For GTM & Sales Teams: Identify verified decision-makers and build targeted prospect lists for faster outreach.

• For Startup Founders: Find customers, partners, investors, and top talent from a single AI-powered platform.

• For Developers: Research companies, explore opportunities, and stay organized with AI-assisted workflows.

• For Analysts: Gather professional and company insights to support research and business decision-making.
Language & Foundation Models — Building AI That Speaks India
Press enter or click to view image in full size

2. Sarvam AI — Multilingual AI for 1 Billion Non-English Indians
Founded by: Vivek Raghavan & Pratyush Kumar (2023)
Category: Language AI | Foundation Models
Sarvam AI is building sovereign, India-first language models with a focus on multilingual coverage and voice-first use. It supports Indian languages across tasks like summarisation, translation, and voice assistants, delivered through APIs and partnerships.
What it does: Sarvam builds AI models that understand and respond in Indian languages — not just Hindi or English, but also Tamil, Telugu, Bengali, and more.
Use Case: A farmer in Odisha receives weather updates and crop advisory in Odia on his phone, powered by Sarvam’s language model — making digital services accessible to those who have never used them in English.
Why it matters: Foundation models rooted in Indian linguistic realities expand the addressable market for every downstream application, reaching hundreds of millions of non-English users.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

3. Krutrim — India’s General-Purpose AI Assistant
Founded by: Bhavish Aggarwal (2024)
Category: Conversational AI | General Purpose Assistant
Spun out of Ola, Krutrim is designed as a general-purpose AI assistant for Indian users, with a focus on Indian languages and cultural context. It aims to feel natural and useful for everyday tasks in India’s linguistic diversity.
What it does: Krutrim understands and responds in multiple Indian languages including Hindi, Tamil, and Telugu alongside English, helping users draft emails, answer questions, and plan tasks.
Use Case: A shopkeeper in Jaipur speaks to Krutrim in Hindi-Hinglish, and it drafts a professional English email for his international suppliers — bridging the language gap in everyday business.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

4. BharatGen AI — India’s First Sovereign AI Initiative
Spearheaded by: IIT Bombay, Department of Science & Technology, Government of India
Category: Sovereign AI | Multilingual Foundation Models
BharatGen is India’s first government-funded multimodal AI initiative — not a single chatbot, but foundational AI infrastructure built for India, by India, and released as a digital public good. Led by IIT Bombay in collaboration with IIT Kanpur, IIT Madras, IIIT Hyderabad, and IIM Indore, it is trained on Bharat Data Sagar — the world’s largest India-centric multilingual dataset — covering 22+ scheduled Indian languages, dialects, and cultural nuances.
What it does: BharatGen delivers a family of open-source AI models — Param2 for reasoning and coding across Indian languages, Shrutam2 for multilingual speech-to-text, Sooktam2 for natural text-to-speech with voice cloning, and Patram for processing Indian government documents and forms.
Use Case: A district government officer uses a BharatGen-powered interface to process citizen grievance forms submitted in a regional dialect — automatically transcribing, summarising, and routing them to the right department, cutting processing time from weeks to hours.
Content, Video & Marketing AI — Performance at Scale
Press enter or click to view image in full size

5. Gan.ai — Personalised Video at Scale
Founded by: Suvrat Bhooshan (2021)
Category: Video AI | Marketing Automation
Gan.ai enables personalised videos at scale. Record one video and it auto-generates thousands of customised versions with each viewer’s name, offer, or location inserted naturally.
What it does: Helps businesses create personalised marketing videos without shooting multiple versions.
Use Case: Zomato records one food delivery ad, and Gan.ai automatically creates a unique version for each customer — “Hi Rahul, here are today’s offers near you in Koramangala.”
Press enter or click to view image in full size

6. Rephrase.ai — Text-to-Avatar Video (Acquired by Adobe)
Founded by: Ashray Malhotra, Nisheeth Lahoti & Shivam Mangla (2018)
Category: Generative Video | Avatar AI
Rephrase.ai pioneered text-to-avatar video, where typed text turns into a lifelike person speaking on screen. The company was acquired by Adobe and its technology is now integrated into mainstream creative tools globally.
What it does: Creates realistic talking-avatar videos directly from text input, eliminating the need for actors or production teams.
Use Case: A bank sends personalised welcome videos to new customers using an AI-generated avatar — reducing production costs while improving the onboarding experience.
Conversational AI & Agentic Work — From Chatting to Doing
Press enter or click to view image in full size

7. Gupshup — The Infrastructure of Business Conversations
Founded by: Beerud Sheth (2005)
Category: Conversational Messaging | Business Communication
Gupshup is the backbone of business conversations on WhatsApp and SMS, powering messaging, chatbots, and transactions for over 100,000 businesses worldwide.
What it does: Helps companies communicate with customers through WhatsApp, SMS, and chat apps, with AI tools to build and manage conversational bots.
Use Case: When you book a movie on BookMyShow and instantly receive your WhatsApp ticket confirmation, that experience is powered by Gupshup.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

8. Haptik & Yellow.ai — Enterprise Conversational AI
Founded by: Haptik — Aakrit Vaish & Swapan Rajdev (2013) | Yellow.ai — Raghu Ravinutala (2016)
Category: Enterprise Chatbots | Voice Automation
Both platforms help large companies automate customer support, sales, and HR through chat and voice bots. Haptik has a strong India presence after joining Reliance Jio, while Yellow.ai has expanded globally with multi-language support.
Use Case: Ordering a Domino’s pizza on WhatsApp or checking your Jio mobile balance with a chatbot — these everyday experiences are powered by platforms like Haptik and Yellow.ai.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

9. Observe.AI — Real-Time Intelligence for Call Centres
Founded by: Swapnil Jain, Akash Singh & Jithendra Vepa
Category: Conversational Intelligence | Contact Centre AI
Observe.AI helps call centres work smarter by listening to conversations, analysing them in real time, and coaching agents live during calls — making every customer interaction more effective.
What it does: Transcribes and analyses customer calls, identifies what is and is not working, and guides agents in real time.
Use Case: While you are speaking with a bank’s call centre agent, Observe.AI is nudging the agent in real time — “Offer a discount now” — making your call faster, smoother, and more likely to resolve successfully.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

10. Veena by Maya Research — Natural Indian Voice AI
Founded by: Maya Research (2026)
Category: Voice AI | Speech Synthesis
Veena is a voice AI that makes digital voices sound natural in Indian languages, especially Hindi and Hinglish. Unlike global AI voices that often sound flat and robotic, Veena is tuned to Indian tones, rhythms, and expressions.
What it does: Generates natural-sounding speech for Hindi, Hinglish, and mixed-language conversations, built for call centres, e-learning platforms, and public services.
Use Case: An online education platform uses Veena to produce clear, natural Hindi audio lessons for students who learn better in their mother tongue.
Final Word
India’s AI ecosystem is no longer playing catch-up — it’s setting the pace. Across industries, Indian startups are building AI products that solve real business challenges, improve everyday productivity, and compete confidently on the global stage. From multilingual foundation models and enterprise automation to AI-powered hiring, marketing, and career intelligence, these innovations prove that world-class AI is being built right here in India.
As AI adoption continues to accelerate, it’s worth looking beyond the familiar global names. Some of the most practical, scalable, and impactful AI solutions are emerging from India’s own startup ecosystem — designed to solve local challenges while creating value worldwide.
If you’re a student exploring your next opportunity, a recruiter hiring top talent, or a business looking to work smarter, platforms like Redrob AI are helping bring hiring, AI-powered search, prospecting, research, and productivity together in one unified workspace.
The future of AI isn’t just being imagined in India — it’s being built here.
Explore Redrob AI and experience a smarter way to work.
